title: Mouse Pancreas Dissection and Fixation for Cryosectioning
author: Islet and Pancreas Analysis Core
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b2fdqbi6

steps.title: Ketamine (15 mg/mL)/xylazine (3 mg/mL) working...
steps.instruction: Ketamine (15 mg/mL)/xylazine (3 mg/mL) working solution – Use 5 mL syringes with 18G needles. Label all bottles with additive, mix date, and expiration date of earliest-expiring ingredient.

steps.title: Pull 2 mL of xylazine and...
steps.instruction: Pull 2 mL of xylazine and inject into a 10 mL bottle of ketamine.

steps.title: Pull 2 mL of ketamine/xylazine and...
steps.instruction: Pull 2 mL of ketamine/xylazine and inject into a 10 mL bottle of saline.

steps.title: Inject mice intraperitoneally with ketamine/xylazine solution...
steps.instruction: Inject mice intraperitoneally with ketamine/xylazine solution (1 μl/mg body weight) using a 1 mL syringe with a 27G needle. Place mice into a plastic bin with air holes.

steps.title: After 5-10 minutes, check to see...
steps.instruction: After 5-10 minutes, check to see if mice are fully anesthetized bygently pinching feet and tail. If there is a jerking or squeaking response, inject about 100 μL more of ketamine/xylazine solution, and wait a few more minutes. Continue checking for full anesthetization before beginning surgery.

steps.title: Coat a labeled cryomold with a...
steps.instruction: Coat a labeled cryomold with a thin layer of OCT compound and set aside.

0.1 M PBS (1L) – Dissolve 12.07 g Na 2 HPO 4 (dibasic), 8.0 g NaCl, and 2.0 g KCl in 1L Milli-Q water. This solution should be made fresh or can be sterile filtered, kept at 4 °C , and used within 1 month.

steps.title: 4% Paraformaldehyde (PFA) – Dilute one...
steps.instruction: 4% Paraformaldehyde (PFA) – Dilute one ampule (10 mL) 16% PFA in 30 mL 0.1M PBS. This solution should be made fresh the day of fixation. Approximately 13mL per pancreas is required.

steps.title: 30% Sucrose – Dissolve 15 g...
steps.instruction: 30% Sucrose – Dissolve 15 g sucrose in 35mL 10 mM PBS. Solution can be placed on shaker to ensure sucrose fully dissolves. Approximately 13 mL per pancreas is required.

steps.title: Pipet 13 mL aliquots into labeled...
steps.instruction: Pipet 13 mL aliquots into labeled 15 mL conical tubes (one per pancreas). Using analytical balance, record weight of each tube to nearest 0.01 g.

steps.title: Place mouse face up on the...
steps.instruction: Place mouse face up on the surgical stage, tape limbs down, and wipe with 70% ethanol. Open the body cavity using forceps and scissors and locate pancreas by moving stomach and intestines to the side. Use two pairs of forceps to separate the pancreas from the duodenum and stomach, starting from the head. Remove the pancreas with the spleen still attached. Above left: Pancreas location relative to other internal organs. Above right: Pancreas tail attaches to spleen; pancreas head attaches to duodenum.

steps.title: Place the pancreas in a 100...
steps.instruction: Place the pancreas in a 100 mm tissue culture dish containing 10 mL ice-cold 10 mM PBS . Dissect away the spleen and remove all fat tissue attached to pancreas. If there is excess bleeding during dissection, rinse pancreas in an additional 10 mL ice-cold 10 mM PBS before fat removal.

steps.title: Place pancreas into preweighed 15 mL...
steps.instruction: Place pancreas into preweighed 15 mL tube with 4% PFA (step 3.1). Using analytical balance, record weight of tube containing pancreas to nearest 0.01 g.

steps.title: Fix pancreas for 02:00:00 on ice...
steps.instruction: Fix pancreas for 02:00:00 on ice with gentle agitation using a shaker at low speed.

steps.title: Decant fixative and wash pancreas 4...
steps.instruction: Decant fixative and wash pancreas 4 times in approximately 13 mL 0.1 M PBS for 00:30:00 . Tubes should be kept on ice for washes with gentle agitation using a shaker at low speed.

steps.title: Decant PBS and add approximately 13 mL 30% sucrose
steps.instruction: Decant PBS and add approximately 13 mL 30% sucrose . Store Overnight at 4 °C to cryoprotect pancreas.

steps.title: Retrieve 15 mL conical tube with...
steps.instruction: Retrieve 15 mL conical tube with pancreas in 30% sucrose (step 11) and pour contents into a 100 mm tissue culture dish.

steps.title: Using fine forceps, pick up pancreas...
steps.instruction: Using fine forceps, pick up pancreas and blot excess sucrose from tissue using Kimwipes.

steps.title: Place the pancreas into the cryomold...
steps.instruction: Place the pancreas into the cryomold as shown below, using forceps to gently push the tissue to the bottom. Orientation of pancreas in cryomold.

steps.title: Place cryomold with pancreas onto a layer of dry ice
steps.instruction: Place cryomold with pancreas onto a layer of dry ice. Fill remaining volume of cryomold with OCT, covering the pancreas, and let freeze thoroughly on dry ice until OCT is opaque.

steps.title: Cut cryosections at 5-10 μm thickness...
steps.instruction: Cut cryosections at 5-10 μm thickness and mount to Plus Gold charged slides for staining. For mass quantification, collect cryosections throughout the entire pancreas and to obtain at least 5 sections 200 μm apart. Immunofluorescent Staining of Mouse Pancreas for Islet Cell Mass Analysis by Islet and Pancreas Analysis Core

steps.title: Wrap frozen cryomold in pre-labeled square...
steps.instruction: Wrap frozen cryomold in pre-labeled square of aluminum foil, place into a sealed plastic bag, and store at -80 °C until ready to cryosection.
